content.problemStatement:
Chef must spend at least 7 days in prison to be released on parole.

Given the number of days he spent, output YES if will be released, otherwise NO.


Input Format
The first and only line of input consists of an integer X denoting the number of days spent in prison by the Chef



Output Format
Output Yes, if the Chef will be released on parole, and No otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YES, yEs, yes, and yeS will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X denoting the number of days Chef has spent in prison.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output "Yes" if X is at least 7, otherwise output "No". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Chef spent only 5 days, which is less than the required 7 days, so he is not released on parole.
Chef spent 12 days, which meets the minimum of 7 days, so he will be released on parole.
First case: 6 < 7 → No. Second case: 7 >= 7 → Yes. Third case: 10 >= 7 → Yes.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read X and compare it with the threshold 7. If X >= 7 print Yes, else print No. This is a direct O(1) check per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.
